title: Setting hosts
text: connect public hosts simply call set_github_host() set_gitlab_host() function without specifying host parameter. wish connect internal GitHub GitLab, need pass names hosts host parameter. Remember also tokens set properly hosts (tokens read ).
code: library(GitStats) git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     orgs = c("r-world-devs", "openpharma"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_PAT")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     orgs = c("mbtests"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITLAB_PAT_PUBLIC")   ) git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     host = "github.internal.com",     orgs = c("org_1", "org_2", "org_3"),     token = Sys.getenv("YOUR_GITHUB_PAT")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     host = "internal.host.com",     orgs = c("internal_org"),     token = Sys.getenv("YOUR_GITLAB_PAT")   )

title: Setting hosts
text: GitStats configured connect GitHub API (version 3) GitLab API (version 4).

title: Setting hosts
text: setting hosts choose scanning scope GitStats : organizations/groups - case need pass character arguments (names organizations (case GitHub) groups (case GitLab)) orgs parameter. repositories - case need pass full names repositories ({org_name}/{repo_name}) repos parameter. organizations/groups repositories - can define time: whole hosts - possible time case private hosts, public ones deemed large. set whole Git platform scanned just set hosts without specifying orgs repos. hand, remember connect internal host, need pass argument host parameter.
code: git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     orgs = c("r-world-devs", "openpharma"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_PAT")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     orgs = c("mbtests"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITLAB_PAT_PUBLIC")   ) git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     repos = c("r-world-devs/GitStats", "r-world-devs/shinyCohortBuilder", "openpharma/DataFakeR"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_PAT")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     repos = "mbtests/gitstatstesting",     token = Sys.getenv("GITLAB_PAT_PUBLIC")   ) git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     orgs = "openpharma",     repos = c("r-world-devs/GitStats", "r-world-devs/shinyCohortBuilder"),     token = Sys.getenv("GITHUB_PAT")   ) git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     host = "github.internal.com",     token = Sys.getenv("YOUR_GITHUB_PAT")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     host = "internal.host.com",     token = Sys.getenv("YOUR_GITLAB_PAT")   )

title: Setting hosts
text: Remember pass set_*_host() functions tokens authorize access. can store tokens environment variables, e.g. defined .Renviron file GITHUB_PAT GitHub GITLAB_PAT GitLab. create tokens refer GitHub API GitLab API documentation. creating tokens asked set access scopes tokens. GitStats work need tokens given scopes: [GitHub] public_repo, read:org read:user, [GitLab] read_api. access tokens stored environment variables names GITHUB_PAT GITHUB_PAT_* GITLAB_PAT GITLAB_PAT_* need specify set_*_host() functions, GitStats automatically find .
code: git_stats <- create_gitstats() |>   set_github_host(     orgs = c("r-world-devs", "openpharma")   ) |>   set_gitlab_host(     orgs = c("mbtests")   )

text: local saving recommend though using SQLite storage. can set set_sqlite_storage() function. , data pulled get_*() functions stored SQLite database retrieved run function . title: Get files — get_files
text: get_files() may used two ways: either pattern (optional depth) file_path argument defined. first scenario, GitStats pull first files structure responding passed pattern depth arguments afterwards files content files. second scenario, GitStats pull content files specific file_path repository. user wants pull particular file files, file_path approach seems reasonable, faster way since omits pulling whole file structure repo. example, user wants pull content README.md /NEWS.md files placed root directories repositories, take file_path approach already knows precisely paths files. hand, user wants pull specific type files (e.g. .md .Rmd files repository), without knowing path, recommended use pattern approach, trigger GitStats find files repository given level directories (pattern argument) afterwards pull content. latter approach slower former may useful depending users' goals. approaches return data format: tibble data files, namely path content.

title: GitStats (development version)
text: set_parallel() is_parallel() now take gitstats object first argument, making pipeable (e.g. gitstats |> set_parallel(4)). Parallel state stored object (#790). Fixed get_repos() with_code failing GitLab code search returns 1000 repositories. GraphQL resolver rejects queries many IDs; get_repos() now detects limit error automatically batches IDs (#781). GitLab repository batching now runs parallel set_parallel() active. Previously, batches fetched sequentially even parallelism enabled (#786). Extracted GraphQL request error-classification logic EngineGraphQL R6 private methods standalone package-level functions, enabling reuse parallel workers without R6 serialization issues. Improvements parallelism visibility: added is_parallel() indicating whether parallel processing currently active public get_*() methods now emit ℹ Running parallel mode. verbose = TRUE (#779). Improved error handling set_*_host() case user doesn’t pass full path repos (#782). Fixed get_files() function (#776).

title: GitStats 2.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-02 release introduces external storage backends (PostgreSQL SQLite) persisting pulled data across sessions, optional parallel processing via mirai faster API calls. also brings several performance improvements, including faster file tree retrieval optimized GitLab repository queries. Added add_languages parameter get_repos() (TRUE default). set FALSE, languages data excluded output GitLab REST languages API calls skipped, speeding process. Fixed get_repos() returning NA commit_sha archived GitLab projects. GraphQL API returns null lastCommit, REST Branches API fallback can now retrieve SHA. Use fill_empty_sha = TRUE get_repos() enable (#746). Added optional parallel processing API calls via mirai package. Use set_parallel() enable concurrent data fetching across repositories organizations (#736). Cached set_owner_type() results avoid redundant GraphQL calls multiple get_* functions used session (#738). Replaced per-directory GraphQL file tree traversal single-call REST recursive tree API get_repos_trees(), substantially improving speed retrieving repository file trees (#740). Added set_postgres_storage(), set_sqlite_storage(), set_local_storage() configure external storage backends. PostgreSQL (via RPostgres/DBI) SQLite (via RSQLite/DBI) supported persisting data database. Metadata (R classes, attributes) preserved via _metadata table (#602). Added remove_from_storage() remove named table active storage backend (#747). Added remove_postgres_storage() remove_sqlite_storage() fully remove database storage backend — PostgreSQL variant drops GitStats schema, SQLite variant deletes database file — revert local storage (#759). Added get_storage_metadata() retrieve metadata (R classes, custom attributes, column types) stored table (#748). Fixed slow get_repos() GitLab specific repos set. Previously, repos_by_user GraphQL query searched entire GitLab instance; now repos queried directly fullPath (#750). Sped vignettes generation (#504, @marcinkowskak).
